description_long: This town hall is a two-part structure featuring a main rectangular building with a gabled red-tiled roof and a smaller, attached annex with a matching roof. The main facade is constructed from gray stone blocks and is adorned with symmetrical rows of tall, shuttered windows, each with yellow wooden shutters. The central entrance is marked by a prominent stone archway and double doors, giving the building a formal civic appearance. The annex, built from a lighter, tan-colored brick, has fewer windows and a simpler design, but complements the main structure. Decorative cornices and horizontal stone bands separate the floors, adding visual interest and a sense of solidity. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional European civic architecture with modest ornamentation and a focus on symmetry.
description: A stately, stone-and-brick town hall with a red-tiled gabled roof, arched entrance, and rows of shuttered windows. The building features a main block and a smaller annex, both with classic architectural details.
description_view_0: The end view shows the gabled roof and the layered stonework of the main building, with the annex partially visible behind.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the full length of the main building and the annex, highlighting the contrasting stone and brick materials and the tiled roofs.
description_view_2: The opposite end view displays the annex's gabled roof and the rear of the main building, emphasizing the stepped rooflines and window placement.
description_view_3: The front view showcases the main entrance with its arched stone doorway, symmetrical shuttered windows, and the annex to the left.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Stone archway at entrance, horizontal stone bands, window shutters, and simple cornices
attribute_entrance_design: Single arched double-door entrance framed by a prominent stone archway, no visible steps or columns, with decorative stonework above the door
